1.english: Whoever Allah guides - he is the [rightly] guided; and whoever He sends astray - it is those who are the losers.
1.transliteration: mai yahdil laahu fa huwal muhtadee wa mai yudlil fa ulaaa'ika humul khaasiroon

1.arabic: ولقد ذرأنا لجهنم كثيرا من الجن والإنس ۖ لهم قلوب لا يفقهون بها ولهم أعين لا يبصرون بها ولهم آذان لا يسمعون بها ۚ أولئك كالأنعام بل هم أضل ۚ أولئك هم الغافلون
1.english: And We have certainly created for Hell many of the jinn and mankind. They have hearts with which they do not understand, they have eyes with which they do not see, and they have ears with which they do not hear. Those are like livestock; rather, they are more astray. It is they who are the heedless.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad zaraanaa li jahannama kaseeram minal jinni wal insi lahum quloobul laa yafqahoona bihaa wa lahum a'yunul laa yubisiroona bihaa wa lahum aazaanul laa yasma'oona bihaa; ulaaa'ika kal an'aami bal hum adall; ulaaa'ika humul ghaafiloon

1.arabic: ولله الأسماء الحسنى فادعوه بها ۖ وذروا الذين يلحدون في أسمائه ۚ سيجزون ما كانوا يعملون
1.english: And to Allah belong the best names, so invoke Him by them. And leave [the company of] those who practice deviation concerning His names. They will be recompensed for what they have been doing.
1.transliteration: Wa lillaahil Asmaaa 'ul Husnaa fad'oohu bihaa wa zarul lazeena yulhidoona feee Asmaaa'ih; sa yujzawna maa kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: وممن خلقنا أمة يهدون بالحق وبه يعدلون
1.english: And among those We created is a community which guides by truth and thereby establishes justice.
1.transliteration: Wa mimman khalaqnaaa ummatuny yahdoona bilhaqqi wa bihee ya'diloon

1.arabic: والذين كذبوا بآياتنا سنستدرجهم من حيث لا يعلمون
1.english: But those who deny Our signs - We will progressively lead them [to destruction] from where they do not know.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena kazzaboo bi Aayaatinaa sanastadrijuhum min haisu laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: وأملي لهم ۚ إن كيدي متين
1.english: And I will give them time. Indeed, my plan is firm.
1.transliteration: Wa umlee lahum; inna kaidee mateen

1.arabic: أولم يتفكروا ۗ ما بصاحبهم من جنة ۚ إن هو إلا نذير مبين
1.english: Then do they not give thought? There is in their companion [Muhammad] no madness. He is not but a clear warner.
1.transliteration: Awalam yatafakkaroo maa bisaahibihim min jinnah; in huwa illaa nazeerum mubeen

1.arabic: أولم ينظروا في ملكوت السماوات والأرض وما خلق الله من شيء وأن عسى أن يكون قد اقترب أجلهم ۖ فبأي حديث بعده يؤمنون
1.english: Do they not look into the realm of the heavens and the earth and everything that Allah has created and [think] that perhaps their appointed time has come near? So in what statement hereafter will they believe?
1.transliteration: Awalam yanzuroo fee malakootis samaawaati wal ardi wa maa khalaqal laahu min shai'inw wa an 'asaaa ai yakoona qadiqtaraba ajaluhum fabi aiyi hadeesim ba'dahoo yu'minoon

1.arabic: من يضلل الله فلا هادي له ۚ ويذرهم في طغيانهم يعمهون
1.english: Whoever Allah sends astray - there is no guide for him. And He leaves them in their transgression, wandering blindly.
1.transliteration: Mai yadlilil laahu falaa haadiyaa lah; wa yazaruhum fee tughyaanihim ya'mahoon

1.arabic: يسألونك عن الساعة أيان مرساها ۖ قل إنما علمها عند ربي ۖ لا يجليها لوقتها إلا هو ۚ ثقلت في السماوات والأرض ۚ لا تأتيكم إلا بغتة ۗ يسألونك كأنك حفي عنها ۖ قل إنما علمها عند الله ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون
1.english: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival? Say, "Its knowledge is only with my Lord. None will reveal its time except Him. It lays heavily upon the heavens and the earth. It will not come upon you except unexpectedly." They ask you as if you are familiar with it. Say, "Its knowledge is only with Allah, but most of the people do not know."
1.transliteration: Yas'aloonaka 'anis Saa'ati aiyaana mursaahaa qul innamaa 'ilmuhaa 'inda Rabbee laa yujallaeehaa liwaqtihaaa illaa Hoo; saqulat fis samaawaati wal ard; laa taateekum illaa baghtah; yas'aloonaka ka annaka hafiyyun 'anhaa qul innamaa 'ilmuhaa 'indal laahi wa laakinna aksaran naasi laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: قل لا أملك لنفسي نفعا ولا ضرا إلا ما شاء الله ۚ ولو كنت أعلم الغيب لاستكثرت من الخير وما مسني السوء ۚ إن أنا إلا نذير وبشير لقوم يؤمنون
1.english: Say, "I hold not for myself [the power of] benefit or harm, except what Allah has willed. And if I knew the unseen, I could have acquired much wealth, and no harm would have touched me. I am not except a warner and a bringer of good tidings to a people who believe."
1.transliteration: Qul laaa amliku linafsee naf'anw wa laa darran illaa maa shaaa'al laah; wa law kuntu a'alamul ghaiba lastaksartu minal khairi wa maa massaniyas soo'; in ana illaa nazeerunw wa basheerul liqawminy yu'minoon

1.arabic: ۞ هو الذي خلقكم من نفس واحدة وجعل منها زوجها ليسكن إليها ۖ فلما تغشاها حملت حملا خفيفا فمرت به ۖ فلما أثقلت دعوا الله ربهما لئن آتيتنا صالحا لنكونن من الشاكرين
1.english: It is He who created you from one soul and created from it its mate that he might dwell in security with her. And when he covers her, she carries a light burden and continues therein. And when it becomes heavy, they both invoke Allah, their Lord, "If You should give us a good [child], we will surely be among the grateful."
1.transliteration: Huwal lazee khalaqakum min nafsinw waahidatinw wa ja'ala minhaa zawjahaa liyas kuna ilaihaa falammaa taghash shaahaa hamalat hamlan khafeefan famarrat bihee falammaaa asqalad da'awal laaha Rabbahumaa la'in aayaitanaa saalihal lanakoo nanna minash shaakireen

1.arabic: فلما آتاهما صالحا جعلا له شركاء فيما آتاهما ۚ فتعالى الله عما يشركون
1.english: But when He gives them a good [child], they ascribe partners to Him concerning that which He has given them. Exalted is Allah above what they associate with Him.
1.transliteration: Falammaaa aataahumaa saalihan ja'alaa lahoo shurakaaa'a feemaaa aataahumaa; fata'aalal laahu 'ammaa yushrikoon

1.arabic: أيشركون ما لا يخلق شيئا وهم يخلقون
1.english: Do they associate with Him those who create nothing and they are [themselves] created?
1.transliteration: A yushrikoona maa laa yakhluqu shai'anw wa hum yukhlaqoon

1.arabic: ولا يستطيعون لهم نصرا ولا أنفسهم ينصرون
1.english: And the false deities are unable to [give] them help, nor can they help themselves.
1.transliteration: Wa laa yastatee'oona lahum nasranw wa laaa anfusahum yansuroon

1.arabic: وإن تدعوهم إلى الهدى لا يتبعوكم ۚ سواء عليكم أدعوتموهم أم أنتم صامتون
1.english: And if you [believers] invite them to guidance, they will not follow you. It is all the same for you whether you invite them or you are silent.
1.transliteration: Wa in tad'oohum ilalhudaa laa yattabi'ookum; sawaaa'un 'alaikum a-da'awtumoohum am antum saamitoon

1.arabic: إن الذين تدعون من دون الله عباد أمثالكم ۖ فادعوهم فليستجيبوا لكم إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: Indeed, those you [polytheists] call upon besides Allah are servants like you. So call upon them and let them respond to you, if you should be truthful.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena tad'oona min doonil laahi 'ibaadun amsaalukum fad'oohum fal yastajeeboo lakum in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: ألهم أرجل يمشون بها ۖ أم لهم أيد يبطشون بها ۖ أم لهم أعين يبصرون بها ۖ أم لهم آذان يسمعون بها ۗ قل ادعوا شركاءكم ثم كيدون فلا تنظرون
1.english: Do they have feet by which they walk? Or do they have hands by which they strike? Or do they have eyes by which they see? Or do they have ears by which they hear? Say, [O Muhammad], "Call your 'partners' and then conspire against me and give me no respite.
1.transliteration: A lahum arjuluny yamshoona bihaa am lahum aidiny yabtishoona bihaaa am lahum a'yunuy yubsiroona bihaaa am lahum aazaanuny yasma'oona bihaa; qulid'oo shurakaaa'akum summa keedooni falaa tunziroon

1.arabic: إن وليي الله الذي نزل الكتاب ۖ وهو يتولى الصالحين
1.english: Indeed, my protector is Allah, who has sent down the Book; and He is an ally to the righteous.
1.transliteration: Inna waliyyial laahul lazee nazzalal Kitaaba wa Huwa yatawallas saaliheen

1.arabic: والذين تدعون من دونه لا يستطيعون نصركم ولا أنفسهم ينصرون
1.english: And those you call upon besides Him are unable to help you, nor can they help themselves."
1.transliteration: Wallazeena tad'oona min doonihee laa yastatee'oona nasrakum wa laaa anfusahum yansuroon

1.arabic: وإن تدعوهم إلى الهدى لا يسمعوا ۖ وتراهم ينظرون إليك وهم لا يبصرون
1.english: And if you invite them to guidance, they do not hear; and you see them looking at you while they do not see.
1.transliteration: Wa in tad'oohum ilal hudaa laa yasm'oo wa taraahum yanzuroona ilaika wa hum laa yubsiroon

1.arabic: خذ العفو وأمر بالعرف وأعرض عن الجاهلين
1.english: Take what is given freely, enjoin what is good, and turn away from the ignorant.
1.transliteration: khuzil 'afwa waamur bil'urfi waa'rid 'anil jaahileen

1.arabic: وإما ينزغنك من الشيطان نزغ فاستعذ بالله ۚ إنه سميع عليم
1.english: And if an evil suggestion comes to you from Satan, then seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is Hearing and Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa immaa yanzaghannaka minash Shaitaani nazghun fasta'iz billaah; innahoo Samee'un Aleem

1.arabic: إن الذين اتقوا إذا مسهم طائف من الشيطان تذكروا فإذا هم مبصرون
1.english: Indeed, those who fear Allah - when an impulse touches them from Satan, they remember [Him] and at once they have insight.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeenat taqaw izaa massahum taaa'ifum minash Shaitaani tazakkaroo fa izaa hum mubsiroon

1.arabic: وإخوانهم يمدونهم في الغي ثم لا يقصرون
1.english: But their brothers - the devils increase them in error; then they do not stop short.
1.transliteration: Wa ikhwaanuhum yamuddoonahum fil ghaiyi summa laa yuqsiroon

1.arabic: وإذا لم تأتهم بآية قالوا لولا اجتبيتها ۚ قل إنما أتبع ما يوحى إلي من ربي ۚ هذا بصائر من ربكم وهدى ورحمة لقوم يؤمنون
1.english: And when you, [O Muhammad], do not bring them a sign, they say, "Why have you not contrived it?" Say, "I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord. This [Qur'an] is enlightenment from your Lord and guidance and mercy for a people who believe."
1.transliteration: Wa izaa lam taatihim bi aayatin qaaloo law lajtabai tahaa; qul innamaaa attabi'u maa yoohaaa ilaiya mir Rabbee; haazaa basaaa'iru mir Rabbikum wa hudanw wa rahmatul liqawminy yu'minoon

1.arabic: وإذا قرئ القرآن فاستمعوا له وأنصتوا لعلكم ترحمون
1.english: So when the Qur'an is recited, then listen to it and pay attention that you may receive mercy.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa quri'al Quraanu fastami'oo lahoo wa ansitoo la 'allakum turhamoon

1.arabic: واذكر ربك في نفسك تضرعا وخيفة ودون الجهر من القول بالغدو والآصال ولا تكن من الغافلين
1.english: And remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear without being apparent in speech - in the mornings and the evenings. And do not be among the heedless.
1.transliteration: Wazkur Rabbaka fee nafsika tadarru'anw wa kheefatanw wa doonal jahri minal qawli bilghuduwwi wal aasali wa laa takum minal ghaafileen

1.arabic: إن الذين عند ربك لا يستكبرون عن عبادته ويسبحونه وله يسجدون ۩
1.english: Indeed, those who are near your Lord are not prevented by arrogance from His worship, and they exalt Him, and to Him they prostrate.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena 'inda Rabbika laa yastakbiroona 'an 'ibaadatihee wa yusabbihoonahoo wa lahoo yasjudoon
